title: Births and Deaths Rate
description:
Segment: 0:26-2:29

From the description: "At standard pressure and 59 °F a metric ton of carbon dioxide gas would fill a sphere 33 feet across (density of CO₂ = 1.87 kg/m³: http://bit.ly/CO2_datasheet). If this is how New York's emissions actually emerged we would see one of these spheres emerge every 0.58 seconds."

visualChannels.comment: The material is animated to show the difference of frequency / rate of births and deaths dynamically. I.e. we see double light-ups in the births.

contextualAttributes.comment: The gravity making the cubes fall is used as a metaphor of the passage of time.
Cosmic / space environment relating to the data theme.
